summary: TASK-20260428-004 did not use a real Generator Claude CLI run; it was implemented directly in the Codex/Analyst session and validated with dry-run/static checks.
details: Do not infer that scripts/run-generator.mjs hardening was itself produced by the Generator pipeline. Real Generator runs remain intentionally separate and require task/{TASK-ID} branch enforcement, JSON handoff, and post-run validation.

summary: Claude CLI --output-format json is not sufficient alone for 02-HARNESS Generator output correctness; pair it with --json-schema and wrapper-side normalization/validation.
details: Claude stdout may be a CLI JSON wrapper whose result field contains the model's structured answer. Store raw stdout separately and write a normalized generator-result.json only after required Generator fields pass validation.

summary: Post-task reusable operational insights need an explicit completion gate, not only session logs or reports.
details: Session logs preserve conversation and reports summarize a task, but future agents need compact reusable guidance. Use logs/insights.jsonl for discoveries that should survive session compaction and be considered during re-entry or handoff.

insight: git branch --merged <target> only works for fast-forward / non-squash merges. With squash merge, branch tips are never ancestors of target so --merged always returns empty for task branches. The correct check is comparing the branch tip tree to the FIRST commit on target whose message contains the task_id (the squash commit itself). Plain git diff target..branch is also misleading once target moves on to other tasks that touch the same files.

Cleanup-style automation should default to dry-run and require an explicit --force for destructive action. The current branch and target branch must be protected at the script level regardless of flag combinations. Each branch verdict should print its evidence (tree hashes, source commit) so users can audit decisions retroactively.

git-branch-policy.md instructs adding post-merge metadata (CURRENT_STATE, session log, MERGE_COMPLETED ledger event) to the squash commit BEFORE creating it. This makes the squash commit's tree strictly larger than the source task branch tip's tree, so the cleanup script's tree-equivalence check refuses to delete the source branch. Two robust fixes: (a) split into two commits on main like TASK-20260428-005 did (pure squash, then post-completion record), or (b) extend the cleanup algorithm to treat metadata-only differences in CURRENT_STATE/session/ledger as merged.

amendment_reason: Backfill category and applied_to_doc per work-history-policy v1.14. Original INS-20260428-006-03 was captured before the category schema existed.

applied_to_doc.section: Post-merge Branch Cleanup → 검증 기준 — Squash 트리 동등성 (Known gotcha note)

insight: Insight categorization (actionable_doc_change / gotcha / proposal / observation) plus an audit gate that blocks TASK_COMPLETED when actionable categories lack applied_to_doc=applied is the simplest enforcement that prevents 'forgotten learnings'. The gate is Tier-2 invariant: applies to all completion attempts equally, no per-task opt-in needed.

resolution_reason: Replace placeholder commit hash with the actual 1st pure squash commit hash on main

applied_to_doc.section: Post-merge Branch Cleanup — Squash Tree Equivalence (Known gotcha note)
